figures.caption: Fig. 1: Architectural Overview of RAVEN. (1) a Data Collection Pipeline that transforms raw web pages and PDFs into a structured format (2) a RAG Engine that indexes the structured data into a vector database for retrieval (3) an Agentic System that takes in the vulnerable code snippet and generates a comprehensive, Google Project Zero Style Vulnerability Report.
Fig. 5: Box Plot Analysis of Overall Scores for all Falcon Models
TABLE II: Comparison of Falcon Models Across RAG Architectures (Overall Score) Soverall

Fig. 6: Falcon H1R-7B Individual Dimension Scores across all RAG Configurations (Abbreviations: E = embeddings-only, H = hybrid retrieval, C = cross-encoder reranker, L = LLM reranker, Ctx=Contextual Chunker)
Fig. 7: CWE Remediation Fix Analysis Plot

Fig. 2: Data Collection Pipeline. (Top) URL extraction and web scraping of security reports; (Bottom) PDF-to-markdown conversion using OCR and Table of Contents (TOC) indexing. The final output is a cleaned, structured markdown for RAG.

Fig. 4: The RAVEN Agentic Pipeline. It orchestrates four specialized agents for end-to-end vulnerability analysis: (1) the Explorer reads the vulnerable code & queries the RAG engine to generate initial findings; (2) the Analyst refines these findings by assessing impact and likelihood; (3) the Reporter synthesizes a three-phase report (vulnerability, exploit, and fix); and (4) the Judge scores the final output against ground truth across four qualitative dimensions
